identity.name: League
identity.description: AI-powered healthcare consumer experience platform that enables payers, providers, and healthcare organizations to deliver personalized digital health engagement solutions.

team.ceo_background: Serial entrepreneur and founder who launched League in 2014 with a mission to empower people with their health every day, leading the company's AI-first transformation strategy.

competitive.market_position: Leading AI-first healthcare CX platform in North America

direct_competitors.name: TELUS Health
direct_competitors.differentiator: Broader corporate wellness solutions vs League's AI-focused engagement platform

direct_competitors.name: Aya Care
direct_competitors.differentiator: Traditional HSA focus vs League's comprehensive AI-powered consumer experience

direct_competitors.name: Alan
direct_competitors.differentiator: European health insurance model vs League's North American B2B platform approach

news.summary: League demonstrates strong momentum with explosive user growth (271% YoY to 63M contracted users) and continued innovation leadership through AI-first strategy. Recent product releases showcase advanced capabilities in orchestrated AI care navigation and multi-agent systems, positioning the company at the forefront of preventive healthcare transformation.

recent_events.event: Launched Spring '26 Release featuring Care Gap Agent Teams and orchestrated AI care navigation
Announced 63M contracted users milestone, 271% YoY growth fueled by AI-first strategy
Unveiled League Agent Teams multi-agent AI system for healthcare consumer needs
Secured $100M USD credit facility from RBCx to support platform development and expansion

nodes.name: Arcadia
nodes.relationship: Partnership for consumer health engagement solutions

fit_score.rationale: League represents an exceptional fit with strong Healthcare sector alignment and clear VC growth strategy match. The company's AI-first approach to healthcare consumer experience addresses a massive $100B market opportunity with proven traction (63M users, 271% YoY growth). Revenue scale of $35M ARR with unicorn valuation trajectory makes this squarely within Sagard's VC mandate rather than PE buyout territory.

confidence.note: Strong data coverage across identity, funding, and competitive landscape, though ecosystem partnerships and team details beyond CEO were limited in sources.
